questions.q: What kind of being was Maricha in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Maricha was a rakshasa, a shape-shifting demon associated with Ravana's clan.

questions.q: Into what form did Maricha transform to lure Rama away?
questions.explanation: Maricha took the shape of a beautiful golden deer to draw Rama away from the hermitage.

questions.q: Whose plan did Maricha carry out by becoming the golden deer?
questions.explanation: Ravana coerced Maricha into the deer disguise as part of his plan to abduct Sita.

questions.q: The golden deer trick was a scheme to make it possible to seize whom?
questions.explanation: The lure was designed so Ravana could carry off Sita while Rama was away.

questions.q: Who was attracted by the golden deer and asked for it to be caught?
questions.explanation: Sita was enchanted by the deer's beauty and asked Rama to capture it for her.

questions.q: Who pursued the golden deer into the forest?
questions.explanation: Rama chased the golden deer at Sita's request, leaving the hermitage.

questions.q: How did Rama finally bring down the golden deer?
options: He shot it with an arrow
He trapped it in a net
He tamed it with a mantra
questions.explanation: Rama pierced the deer with an arrow, after which it revealed its true rakshasa form.

questions.q: As he was struck down, Maricha cried out in whose voice?
questions.explanation: Maricha imitated Rama's voice, crying out for Sita and Lakshmana to deceive them.

questions.q: Maricha was a relative of which demon king?
questions.explanation: Maricha was a kinsman of Ravana, the rakshasa king of Lanka.

questions.q: Where did the golden deer episode take place?
options: In the forest during Rama's exile
questions.explanation: The episode occurred in the Dandaka forest while Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana were in exile.

questions.q: Who was Maricha's mother, the demoness Rama had earlier opposed?
questions.explanation: Maricha was the son of the demoness Tataka, whom Rama slew in his youth.

questions.q: In which earlier episode had Rama first wounded or repelled Maricha?
options: During the building of the bridge to Lanka
questions.explanation: Rama struck Maricha while guarding the sage Vishwamitra's yajna, flinging him far away with an arrow.

questions.q: With which special weapon did Rama hurl Maricha far across the land during Vishwamitra's sacrifice?
questions.explanation: Rama used the Manavastra (Manava weapon) to fling Maricha a great distance into the sea.

questions.q: Why was Maricha at first reluctant to attack Rama again as the deer?
options: He had already suffered Rama's power and feared him
He was bound by a vow of non-violence
He had lost his shape-shifting ability
questions.explanation: Having been hurled away by Rama before, Maricha dreaded Rama's might and warned Ravana against the plan.

questions.q: What advice did Maricha give Ravana about Sita and Rama?
options: To abandon the abduction plan and not provoke Rama
To make peace and offer tribute
To seek a boon from Brahma first
questions.explanation: Maricha repeatedly counseled Ravana to give up enmity with Rama, warning it would doom Lanka.

questions.q: Why did Maricha ultimately agree to become the golden deer despite his warnings?
options: He was promised half of Lanka
He wished to avenge his mother
questions.explanation: Maricha reasoned that death by Rama's hand was better than death at the hands of the enraged Ravana, so he obeyed.

questions.q: What effect did Maricha's cry in Rama's voice have on Sita?
options: She feared Rama was in danger and sent Lakshmana to help
She ran into the forest herself
questions.explanation: Believing Rama was hurt, Sita urged a reluctant Lakshmana to go to Rama's aid, leaving her alone.

questions.q: Who was left guarding Sita when the golden deer first appeared, before being sent away?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana was guarding Sita but was sent after Rama once she heard the false cry for help.

questions.q: Lakshmana suspected the cry for help was a trick because he believed what about Rama?
options: That no demon could truly endanger Rama
questions.explanation: Lakshmana argued that Rama was invincible and the cry must be a demon's deception, but yielded to Sita's insistence.

questions.q: What distinctive appearance made the golden deer so alluring?
options: A golden body flecked with jewel-like spots
questions.explanation: Maricha's deer form had a shining golden hide dappled with silver or jewel-like spots, dazzling to behold.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, the slaying of which deer is sometimes counted among the cunning of the rakshasa clan?
options: The golden deer that was Maricha
questions.explanation: The golden deer was Maricha in disguise, a trick of the rakshasa Ravana's design.

questions.q: After Maricha's death and the false cry, what calamity immediately followed?
questions.explanation: With Sita left unguarded, Ravana disguised as a mendicant carried her off to Lanka.

questions.q: In Valmiki's Ramayana, how many times had Maricha previously encountered Rama's power before the deer episode?
questions.explanation: Maricha recalled being defeated by Rama twice, including being hurled into the sea, which deepened his terror.

questions.q: Which mark on the golden deer did Valmiki describe as making it irresistibly beautiful?
options: Spots like scattered silver and jewels over a golden coat
A single emerald set in its brow
questions.explanation: Valmiki describes the deer as golden with silver and jewel-like spots and tips of sapphire-like hue.

questions.q: According to tradition, what state did Maricha attain by dying at Rama's hands?
options: Liberation (moksha) through death by the Lord
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that dying by Rama's arrow granted Maricha liberation, a grace despite his demonic life.

questions.q: In Maricha's earlier life as an ascetic-turned-demon, with which two companions did he often disrupt sages' sacrifices?
questions.explanation: Maricha, with Subahu and his mother Tataka, harassed the sages, prompting Vishwamitra to seek Rama's help.

questions.q: When Rama first defeated Maricha at Vishwamitra's hermitage, what happened to Maricha's companion Subahu?
options: Subahu was killed by a fiery weapon
Subahu was also turned into a deer
Subahu surrendered and became a sage
questions.explanation: Rama slew Subahu with the Agneyastra (fire weapon) while sparing Maricha by hurling him far away with the Manavastra.

questions.q: What did Maricha foresee would be the consequence for Ravana if the abduction proceeded?
options: The destruction of Ravana and Lanka
The loss of Ravana's flying chariot
questions.explanation: Maricha prophetically warned that abducting Sita would bring about Ravana's ruin and Lanka's destruction.

questions.q: Where had Maricha been living in seclusion when Ravana came to enlist him for the deer plan?
options: As an ascetic in a forest hermitage by the sea
questions.explanation: Fearful of Rama, Maricha had withdrawn to live as an ascetic in a forest retreat when Ravana sought him out.

questions.q: What was the deeper strategic purpose of Maricha imitating Rama's voice as he died?
options: To draw Lakshmana away so Sita would be left wholly alone
To summon Ravana to the spot
To curse Rama with his last breath
questions.explanation: By mimicking Rama's cry, Maricha ensured Lakshmana would leave Sita, completing the conditions for her abduction.
